description:
# Magisk on WSA (with Google Apps)

## Features
- Integrate Magisk and OpenGApps in a few clicks within minutes
- No Linux environment required for integration
- Keep each build up to date
- Support both ARM64 and x64
- Support all OpenGApps variants except for aroma (aroma does not support x86_64, please use super instead)
- Fix external storage access of DocumentUI
- Unattended installation
- Automatically activates developers mode in Windows 11
- Update to new version while preserving data with one-click script
- Merged all language packs
- Support managing start menu icons (manually installing [WSAHelper](https://github.com/LSPosed/WSAHelper/releases/latest) to use this feature)

### Build sources

Downloading artifacts from GitHub without authentication is not allowed, so a third party redirector, called nightly.link generates the download links for the package. The files are downloaded directly from GitHub's servers.
Builds created in the main LSposed/MagiskOnWSA repo can be outdated, so some developer's forks are also used as a build source. These devs are members of the LSPosed organization on GitHub. For details, see `update.ps1`, as described in the next section.

#### Package updates

New versions of this package are published automatically via the `update.ps1` script (viewable in the package source on GitHub).
summary: Integrate Magisk root and Google Apps (OpenGApps) into WSA (Windows Subsystem for Android)
author: yujincheng08
